pr.title: itdosei: Remove unnecessary stack pad, remove unnecessary const, cleanup function names and add GET_* usage
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleanup-focused itDosei PR that renamed several remaining generic it_3F14, it_8028xxxx, and fn_8028xxxx symbols to the item-specific itDosei_* namespace, updated the Dosei item logic table and symbols.txt accordingly, removed an unnecessary const zero float, removed at least one unnecessary stack pad, and replaced direct gobj field access with GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ where matching allowed. The changes improve symbol clarity and item-local naming consistency without evidence of behavioral changes.
what_changed: Renamed Dosei logic callbacks from the generic it_3F14_Logic7_* namespace to item-specific names such as itDosei_Logic7_Spawned, itDosei_Logic7_PickedUp, itDosei_Logic7_Dropped, itDosei_Logic7_Thrown, itDosei_Logic7_EnteredAir, itDosei_Logic7_DmgReceived, and itDosei_Logic7_DmgDealt.

Renamed generic helper symbols such as fn_80281390, fn_80281734, fn_80282CD4, it_802817A0, it_80281C6C, it_80282074, it_80282BFC, and it_80282DE4 to itDosei_80281390, itDosei_80281734, itDosei_80282CD4, itDosei_802817A0, itDosei_80281C6C, itDosei_80282074, itDosei_80282BFC, and itDosei_80282DE4.
Updated the Dosei entry in src/melee/it/it_3F14.c's item logic table to use the new itDosei_Logic7_* callback names.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt so the linker/symbol map names match the renamed C functions at the same addresses.
Updated src/melee/it/items/itdosei.h declarations to match the renamed functions.
Removed the file-scope const f32 it_804DC878 = 0.0f and replaced its uses in itDosei_FacingAngle calls with the literal 0.0f.
Replaced several direct accesses like gobj->user_data and gobj->hsd_obj with GET_ITEM(gobj) and GET_JOBJ(gobj), where shown in the diff.

Simplified some decomp-shaping code, including removing an unnecessary PAD_STACK(8) in itDosei_Logic7_Spawned and collapsing an artificial scoped int not_msid_4 temporary into a direct if (ip->msid != 4) check.
smart_moves: Kept symbols.txt, the public header, implementation names, and the it_3F14 item logic table synchronized, avoiding stale symbol references after renaming.
Used the item-specific itDosei_* prefix for previously generic addresses, making call sites and callback tables easier to search by item.
Removed a dedicated const-zero sdata symbol once it was not needed for matching, reducing artificial decomp artifacts while preserving the same call argument value.
Applied GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ macros in places that still matched, aligning the file with common project style and making object access more explicit.

Cleaned matching scaffolding incrementally rather than changing behavior: callback wiring, function bodies, and collision/animation flow appear preserved while names and minor expression forms were improved.
decomp_lessons: When a generic item-bank symbol is confidently tied to a concrete item, rename it across the C file, header, logic table, and symbols.txt in the same PR.
A temporary sdata const such as a standalone 0.0f can sometimes be replaced by a literal once matching no longer requires preserving the symbol.
GET_ITEM(gobj) and GET_JOBJ(gobj) are preferred readability/style macros when they do not disturb the match.
Artificial locals and scopes introduced for matching, such as a one-use boolean/int temporary around an if condition, should be removed once a cleaner expression still matches.
Unnecessary PAD_STACK calls should be revisited during cleanup; some stack pads may be historical matching aids that are no longer required.
Function-pointer callback sites are high-value rename targets because table entries document an item's lifecycle behavior and make item logic easier to navigate.
naming_conventions: Use the concrete item prefix itDosei_ for Dosei item functions instead of generic it_3F14_, it_8028xxxx, or fn_8028xxxx names when the function ownership is known.

Lifecycle callback names follow the pattern itDosei_Logic7_<Event>, with events shown in this PR including Spawned, PickedUp, Dropped, Thrown, EnteredAir, DmgReceived, DmgDealt, Reflected, Clanked, and HitShield.
Unidentified motion callbacks retain the existing itDosei_UnkMotionN_{Anim,Phys,Coll} pattern.
Address-suffixed names such as itDosei_802817A0 remain acceptable for item-owned functions whose semantic name is not yet known.
Header prototypes and symbols.txt entries should mirror implementation names exactly after a rename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed an unnecessary PAD_STACK(8) in itDosei_Logic7_Spawned while preserving the function's behavior.
Removed the const f32 zero symbol it_804DC878 and used literal 0.0f at itDosei_FacingAngle call sites, indicating the literal form matched or was otherwise no longer an obstacle.
Converted some direct field reads to GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ macros without changing generated behavior in the shown diff.
Simplified rotation reset code by removing an extra artificial block around HSD_JObjSetRotationX/Y/ZWithMtxDirty calls.
Collapsed a temporary not_msid_4 variable into a direct ip->msid != 4 conditional, a common cleanup once the compiler output remains compatible.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no human text excerpt was provided, so no specific reviewer guidance can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other item files with generic it_3F14_Logic* names that can be renamed to item-specific prefixes.
Search for remaining fn_8028xxxx symbols in item files that have clear item ownership.
Search for const f32 zero globals used only as 0.0f arguments and verify whether they can be removed without breaking the match.
Audit direct gobj->user_data and gobj->hsd_obj access in item code for safe GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ macro cleanup.
Check remaining PAD_STACK calls in itdosei.c to distinguish required matching pads from removable artifacts.
